data.title: Congress approved only a fraction of Trump's proposed budget cuts in recent deals, rejecting the majority.
data.description: Lawmakers have made significant decisions over the last three months by dismissing several of the president's proposals aimed at reducing the size of the government.

data.title: A start-up in the Bay Area is using tech billboards to target specific audiences, sparking curiosity.
data.description: Billboards that may seem perplexing to those not in the tech sector are now prevalent in the Bay Area. A particular company has elevated the microtargeting strategy to new heights.

data.title: ITC Infotech sets up Asia-Pacific headquarters & Digital & AI Engineering Hub in Melbourne, Australia.
data.description: In Melbourne, Australia, ITC Infotech has recently opened its Asia-Pacific headquarters and Digital & AI Engineering Hub in Bangalore, India. This move marks a significant step in the company's expansion efforts in the region.

data.title: IREN appoints John Gross as Chief Innovation Officer. Leader in high-density data center engineering and liquid cooling.
data.description: A prominent industry player excels in high-density data center engineering and liquid cooling solutions on a global scale. Specializing in creating innovative and efficient data center configurations, this company has set itself apart as a leader in the field. Their expertise in managing high-density data centers and implementing liquid cooling techniques has garnered widespread recognition within the industry. With an impressive track record of successful projects, they have solidified their position as a go-to choice for businesses seeking cutting-edge data center solutions.

data.title: Portland General Electric is acquiring PacifiCorp's Washington utility operations for $1.9B, expects to boost EPS. 2025 financial results: $306M net income GAAP, $336M non-GAAP, $2.77 EPS. Initiating 2026 adjusted earnings guidance: $3.33 to $3.53 per diluted share. PGE will construct two solar and battery hybrid projects for 615 MW and reaffirms 5-7% long-term EPS growth. Conference call scheduled on February 17, 2026 at 8:00am ET. Find more information at https://www.manulifeim.com/institutional/us/en. PGE plans to file acknowledgement of the final shortlist of bidders for the 2025 All-source RFP with the OPUC on February 17, 2026. Quarterly dividend announced.
data.description: Portland General Electric (PGE) announced a $1.9 billion acquisition of select Washington state generation, transmission, and electric utility operations from PacifiCorp. This includes three generation facilities and 4,500 miles of transmission and distribution lines, benefiting 140,000 Washington customers. The acquisition is expected to enhance PGE's long-term EPS and dividend growth. PGE will partner with Manulife Investment Management for this venture. Additionally, agreements were made to construct two solar and battery hybrid projects, totaling 615 MW. PGE reported full-year 2025 GAAP financial results of $2.77 per diluted share and non-GAAP results of $3.05 per diluted share. The company also announced 2026 adjusted earnings guidance of $3.33 to $3.53 per diluted share. PGE plans to file a request for acknowledgment of the final shortlist of bidders for the 2025 All-source RFP. The company outlined its 2026 earnings guidance assumptions and discussed quarterly dividends. The webcast replay is available on PGE's investor website. Non-GAAP financial measures were discussed, excluding certain items for a comparative analysis of the Company's performance trends. The forward-looking statement included potential risks and uncertainties related to the implemented acquisition. PGE strives to meet emission reduction targets and customers’ evolving energy needs. They are excelling in renewable energy prioritization and have high customer satisfaction, financial contributions, and volunteer hours towards community welfare.

data.title: Meituan expects to report a loss of up to 24.3 billion yuan in 2025, with a fourth-quarter loss of about 15.7 billion yuan. China's market regulator is trying to ease competition among Meituan, Alibaba, and JD.com in the instant commerce sector.
data.description: China's leading online-to-offline company, Meituan, is expected to report a loss of up to 24.3 billion yuan for 2025, with a fourth-quarter loss of about 15.7 billion yuan. This marks an improvement from its third-quarter loss of $2.7 billion. Competing with e-commerce giants Alibaba and JD.com in the instant commerce sector led to Meituan spending billions on subsidies. While the company's losses have slightly improved, the competition remains intense, prompting China's market regulator to intervene and try to ease the competition among major internet companies. Meituan's struggles in the market are highlighted by its significant losses, reflecting the challenging landscape of the O2O services industry in China.

data.title: Ooma launches AI-powered POTSTracker.com to help orgs manage POTS line discontinuance as carriers retire legacy voice services.
data.description: Ooma, Inc., a Sunnyvale-based company specializing in advanced communication services, unveiled POTSTracker.com. This new platform leverages artificial intelligence to monitor and analyze the discontinuation of legacy voice services, aiding businesses in adapting to the changing landscape. With telecom carriers in the US expediting the retirement of traditional copper-based Plain Old Telephone Service (POTS) lines, POTSTracker.com offers organizations valuable insights and support. This initiative addresses the challenges posed by the phasing out of POTS lines and aims to facilitate a smoother transition for businesses and consumers.

data.title: Endava partners with Miro to streamline AI workflows, enhance global operations, and improve decision cycles.
data.description: Endava, a technology-driven business transformation group based in London, has partnered with Miro®, an AI Innovation Workspace provider, to introduce collaborative AI workflows to its global operations. This partnership aims to enhance Endava's AI-native delivery model, Dava.Flow™. The implementation of Miro across Endava's enterprise will help in speeding up decision-making processes and fostering better alignment among distributed teams. This collaboration signifies Endava's commitment to innovation and leveraging cutting-edge technology.

data.title: Proofpoint launches Partner Network to help partners grow faster and deliver greater value to customers.
data.description: Proofpoint, Inc., based in Sunnyvale, California, has introduced the Proofpoint Partner Network. This new global partner program is aimed at assisting partners in accelerating their growth, boosting margins, and providing enhanced value to customers amidst rising cyber threats on individuals, data, and AI-driven processes. The network features a streamlined framework and increased investments tailored to customer purchasing patterns.
